mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint combination of rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) produces deeply negative real interest rates, which reduce real discount rates applied to future earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound monetary regime, month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal durable demand recovery and justify higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, positive RSAFS surprises fail to coincide with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation exceeding 1.0% over six months (CPIAUCSL) signals impending monetary tightening, which raises the equity discount rate and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 1.0% over the prior 6 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered CPIAUCSL accelerations are not negative in more than 45% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and consumption capacity, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS do not coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% over three months signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST declines are followed by negative 20-day returns in fewer than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 0.5% signals an aggregate demand shock that outpaces prior expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises fail to show a positive rank correlation with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.
